description_long: This is a heavy-duty sledgehammer featuring a robust, rectangular black metal head with flat striking faces on both ends. The head is solid and thick, designed for delivering powerful blows, and shows a slightly worn, matte finish. The handle is long, cylindrical, and made of natural wood, displaying a light tan color with visible grain patterns and some surface scuffs. The handle is straight and tapers slightly toward the end, providing leverage for two-handed use. The connection between the head and handle appears secure, with a metal wedge visible on the top face to hold the head in place. This tool is typically used for demolition, breaking concrete, or driving stakes in urban construction or maintenance settings.
description: A heavy sledgehammer with a black rectangular metal head and a long, straight wooden handle showing visible grain and wear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the rectangular black metal head.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the circular end of the wooden handle and the width of the hammer head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the length of the handle and the thickness of the hammer head.
description_view_3: End-on view of the hammer head, displaying the metal wedge securing the handle and the flat striking face.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle, cylindrical, light tan with visible grain and minor surface scuffs, no ergonomic grip or texturing.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular sledgehammer head, solid steel, with two flat striking faces and a central wedge securing the handle.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible surface scuffs and minor wear on the wooden handle, slight dulling on the metal head, but no major damage.
